headline: IBM Pushes Dow to First Record Since February
summary: The Dow clawed its way back to a new record Thursday with a big boost from IBM.  IBM stock jumped by about 12% on news the tech company is going to potentially receive $1 billion in funding from the U.S. to establish a new quantum foundry subsidiary.  Just before the closing bell, IBM's stock had added 153 points to the Dow.

headline: IBM, Quantum Stocks Jump After Trump Administration Awards $2 Billion
summary: IBM stock and shares of several quantum computing firms jumped Thursday after the U.S. Commerce Department announced $2 billion in grants to advance the technology.  Chipmaker Global Foundries will receive $375 million.  "These strategic quantum technology investments will build on our domestic industry, creating thousands of high-paying American jobs while advancing American quantum capabilities," Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said in a news release, published by IBM.

headline: US to award $2 billion to quantum computing firms and take equity stakes, WSJ reports
summary: The Trump administration is awarding $2 billion in grants to nine quantum-computing companies in deals ‌that include the U.S. government taking equity stakes, ‌the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing the Commerce Department.  The ​department has agreed to give $1 billion of the package to IBM, while GlobalFoundries will receive $375 million, according to the report.  The remaining firms, including D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing and Infleqtion, ‌are expected to receive $100 ⁠million each, while startup Diraq may receive $38 million, according to the WSJ report.

The ​department has agreed to give $1 billion of the package to IBM, while GlobalFoundries will receive $375 million, according to the report.  The remaining firms, including D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing and Infleqtion, ‌are expected to receive $100 ⁠million each, while startup Diraq may receive $38 million, according to the WSJ report.
